core_mechanism: Weighted-L^2 (generalized least-squares) orthogonal projection of a function onto a fixed basis via the Gram/moment-matrix normal equations, where a change of the weighting measure makes the basis non-orthogonal (off-diagonal Gram entries) so that a diagonal, orthogonality-assuming shortcut departs from the true projection by an excess squared-error given exactly as a quadratic form in the Gram matrix
brief_reason: The abstract frames the method as a weighted-L^2 projection in which the basis's Gram matrix is diagonal under one measure but gains off-diagonal terms under another, and computes the exact excess L^2 risk of the diagonal rule as a moment quadratic form solved via Hankel-Cholesky factorizations.
